page: Amazon Web Services hpc8a.96xlarge is a high-performance computing instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs using the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R45 processor, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data places this instance in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 38,653.15 GB/sec for cached reads and 1,067.63 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, database transactions, and LLM prompt processing. However, LLM text generation speeds for 135M and 7B models fall into the average tier, and there is no local storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached resources.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, supporting intensive distributed workloads. Given the dedicated core allocation, high clock speed, and massive memory bandwidth, the hpc8a.96xlarge is engineered for high-performance computing (HPC) applications, large-scale scientific simulations, software compilation, and complex parallel processing tasks that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc8a.96xlarge is a high-performance computing instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance, ranking in the top 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and database benchmarks. With a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, it is highly optimized for compute-intensive workloads. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, representing a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is best suited for high-performance computing, scientific simulations, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc8a.96xlarge is a high-performance computing instance with 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 300 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for compute-intensive parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 192-core high-performance computing platform featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and 300 Gbps network bandwidth for intensive parallel processing.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz.
